title: Unexpected warning about unconsumed build arguments TARGETARCH TARGETOS TARGETPLATFORM
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Hi,

It's not a big problem, but podman 4.3 and 4.4 currently warns about unconsumed build arguments TARGETARCH TARGETOS TARGETPLATFORM when doing multi-arch builds. This warning is useful for user-defined arguments, but the implicit ones that are use for multi-arch builds should not yield such warnings, it's totally fine not to use them if not needed.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

This was run from the  `quay.io/podman/upstream` image:
```
[root@27fbde6be76d /]# mkdir /src
[root@27fbde6be76d /]# echo "FROM quay.io/fedora/fedora" > /src/Dockerfile
[root@27fbde6be76d /]# podman build --platform=Linux/amd64,Linux/arm64 /src
[linux/amd64] STEP 1/1: FROM quay.io/fedora/fedora
Trying to pull quay.io/fedora/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 49822917e40a done
Copying config 885d2b38b8 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
WARN[0032] Ignoring global metacopy option, not supported with booted kernel 3.10.0-1160.76.1.el7.x86_64 containers/podman#1 SMP Tue Jul 26 14:15:37 UTC 2022
[linux/amd64] COMMIT
--> 885d2b38b81
[Warning] one or more build args were not consumed: [TARGETARCH TARGETOS TARGETPLATFORM] <--- This warning is unexpected.
[linux/arm64] STEP 1/1: FROM quay.io/fedora/fedora
885d2b38b8195fef1151979cad84d033484c669e9b7ff803b42df6f165b62aa8
Trying to pull quay.io/fedora/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b4231d545c3c done
Copying config b17fd3ae66 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
WARN[0047] Ignoring global metacopy option, not supported with booted kernel 3.10.0-1160.76.1.el7.x86_64 containers/podman#1 SMP Tue Jul 26 14:15:37 UTC 2022
[linux/arm64] COMMIT   
--> b17fd3ae66c
[Warning] one or more build args were not consumed: [TARGETARCH TARGETOS TARGETPLATFORM]   <--- This warning is unexpected.
b17fd3ae66c33fc5478dcb2dcdec0c77ec789fa5c45db26892e9c537fbaa73b4
```

**Describe the results you received:**

There is an expected warning: `[Warning] one or more build args were not consumed: [TARGETARCH TARGETOS TARGETPLATFORM]`.


**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman should not warned about unconsumed build arguments TARGETARCH TARGETOS TARGETPLATFORM.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@27fbde6be76d /]# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.4.0-dev
API Version:  4.4.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @Romain-Geissler-1A!  Really cool you're testing development version.

@flouthoc, can you take a look?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@flouthoc Did you get a chance to look at this?
I have the same issue with 4.0.0-dev.
I'll check this moving this to buildah.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
